message:
feat(analytics): Track B repair path via rebuild_token_daily_stats [m7xrw]

Mock-finder bead m7xrw surfaced that `cass analytics validate --fix`
could DETECT Track B rollup drift but could not REPAIR any Track B
failure automatically — every track_b.* classification fell through to
`RepairKind::TrackAllRebuildUnavailable` with reason "Track B or
cross-track repair needs a track-all rebuild path that is not
implemented yet", even though the building blocks were already in
place:

  - FrankenStorage::rebuild_token_daily_stats() replays the intact
    `token_usage` ledger into fresh `token_daily_stats` rows inside
    a transaction (src/storage/sqlite.rs:9174).
  - The existing --fix plumbing at src/lib.rs:4780+ already handled
    RepairKind::RebuildTrackA end-to-end.

This commit wires the Track B repair path end-to-end:

1. src/analytics/validate.rs: new RepairKind::RebuildTrackB variant.
   classify_repair_kind() now routes `track_b.has_data`,
   `track_b.grand_total_match`, `track_b.tool_calls_match`,
   `track_b.non_negative_counters`, and any future same-shape
   track_b.* checks to RebuildTrackB. Infrastructure-level failures
   that rebuild_token_daily_stats cannot fix on its own
   (`track_b.tables_exist`, `track_b.agents_table_missing`,
   `track_b.query_exec`) correctly stay as
   TrackAllRebuildUnavailable with an improved reason string
   directing operators to `cass index --full --force-rebuild`.

2. src/analytics/validate.rs: RepairPlan gains `apply_track_b_rebuild`
   as a companion to `apply_track_a_rebuild`. `build_repair_plan`
   marks RebuildTrackB decisions as `fixable: true`.

3. src/lib.rs: new match arm for RepairKind::RebuildTrackB in the
   --fix execution loop. Opens FrankenStorage, calls
   rebuild_token_daily_stats(), emits a `rebuild_track_b` entry to
   applied_repairs with `token_daily_stats_rows`. On failure, defers
   the check_ids and surfaces a `rebuild_track_b_failed` entry
   directing operators toward full canonical replay — matches the
   shape of the Track A failure path.

4. src/lib.rs: --fix JSON envelope gains a top-level `tracks_rebuilt`
   field (BTreeSet-ordered subset of ["a","b"]) so downstream
   consumers can tell at a glance which rebuild paths actually ran
   without grepping applied_repairs[].kind.

5. src/analytics/validate.rs: replaced the previous
   `repair_plan_marks_track_b_failures_as_unavailable` test (which
   asserted the old deferred behavior) with TWO regression tests:
     - `repair_plan_marks_track_b_data_drift_as_rebuild_track_b`
       pins: DELETE FROM token_daily_stats + token_usage intact
       → RebuildTrackB + fixable=true.
     - `repair_plan_marks_track_b_tables_missing_as_unavailable`
       pins: DROP TABLE token_usage → TrackAllRebuildUnavailable
       + fixable=false + track_b.tables_exist present.

Scope note: cross_track.drift classification is intentionally
left alone. Mixed cross-track drift with "Track B higher" semantics
already maps to RebuildTrackA (the existing logic). Other cross-track
scenarios still defer — full canonical replay from messages is the
ibuuh.29 / z9fse.13 class of work that this commit does NOT implement.

Verified: cargo test --lib analytics:: passes 176/176 (including 22
analytics::validate::* tests, the new regression pair, and all
existing rebuild_token_daily_stats unit tests).

Refs coding_agent_session_search-m7xrw

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
